claims.value: 1,464 present for duty, June 30 (114 officers + 1,350 men); tablet carries 'about 1,470'

references.interpretation: The engaged-strength primary (dossier EC2 closed pass 11); the tablet's 1,470 'present about' is the same population under a looser measure.
Carries '820 of 1,470' — the commission's arithmetic; both figures carried, the return is primary.

claims.note: Macro track t=0 carries 1,464; the slice start strength is the macro interpolation at t0 (R-strength-interp-endpoints).

claims.value: Launched from the Oak Hill ground at the formation tablet; leading brigade of the division's attack, on the division right

references.interpretation: Formation-semantics tablet position (ED-27 verification).
Formed on the division right; launched on Rodes's order when the enemy came out to the hill's foot.

claims.value: The division attacked at 2 P.M. (tablet); the destruction window CA-J1P-2 adopted 14:30-15:00, envelope 14:15-15:15

references.excerpt: made a vigorous attack at 2 P.M. with superior numbers along the entire line
references.interpretation: The 14:00 attack clock; the interior ladder (Coulter 12:30 general firing, Cutler's 2 o'clock support sync) brackets it.

references.excerpt: about 12.30 p.m. the firing became general along the entire line
references.interpretation: Interior Union clock below the tablet's 14:00.
The 2 o'clock right-support sync from the receiving line (CA-J1P-1 preamble).

claims.note: The slice window t=5820..7050 (14:37-14:57) sits inside the adopted destruction bracket.

claims.value: Drawn mid-advance on the 2 P.M. sheet: red 'IVERSON, N.C.' bar with regimental numbers, 408 m from the formation tablet along the advance axis toward the Forney-field wall

references.interpretation: The drawn mid-advance state; carried at its macro keyframe time t=4500 (14:15). The bar's regimental numbers (23, 20, 5 ...) are the slice's only drawn regimental-order evidence.

claims.note: Outside the slice window (t0=5820); anchors the pre-window connective route and the regimental order editorial decision EDI-5.

claims.value: Advanced unsupported: the gap on the left never filled (O'Neal repulsed 'almost instantaneously'), Daniel's promised support formed elsewhere

references.interpretation: The support-miscarriage ledger in the commander's own report.
O'Neal's repulse from the division frame.

claims.note: Why the left of the line dies: the wall's fire is unanswered on that flank. Drives the film's left-to-right destruction gradient.

claims.value: Advanced to within 100 yards of the enemy, strongly posted in woods and behind a concealed stone wall

references.interpretation: The standoff distance of the death line from the wall; the slice authors the halted line ~90 m NW of the wall trace (EDI-1).

claims.value: A most desperate fight took place at the 100-yard line; the brigade fought from where it lay

references.excerpt: a most desperate fight took place
references.interpretation: The brigade returned fire through the destruction — unlike the Angle slice's ED-23, friendly musketry and reload cycles are in-window here.
Sustained exchange attested from the receiving side.

claims.value: The line of battle lay down in position under the fire and held its alignment; Iverson read the raised handkerchiefs against 'my line of battle still lying down in position'

references.excerpt: my line of battle still lying down in position
references.interpretation: The men fought prone/behind the swale. REPRESENTED since the fight-prone-vocab slice: segments carrying this claim compile to the fight_prone action class (go-prone under fire, prone fire cycle with the attested roll-to-load compromise, prone deaths settling where the man lay) — T5 vocabulary gap #1 closed; docs/reconstruction/fight-prone-vocab.md carries the evidence.

claims.value: 500 dead and wounded left lying on a line as straight as a dress parade; the dead lay in a distinctly marked line of battle

references.excerpt: 500 of my men were left lying dead and wounded on a line as straight as a dress parade
references.interpretation: The exemplar: killed-and-wounded concentrated on the halted line itself. Falls happen in place; bodies persist; the formation gaps ARE the record.

references.excerpt: His dead lay in a distinctly marked line of battle
references.interpretation: Independent line-geometry corroboration from the division commander.
The rescuer's summary of the brigade's state.

claims.value: ANV return p. 342: 5th NC 31k+112w=143, 12th NC 10+46=56, 20th NC 29+93=122, 23rd NC 41+93=134; missing pooled at brigade 308; printed total row fails its own arithmetic (ED-71)

references.interpretation: Regiment-grain killed+wounded shape: the left three regiments carry ~2.5-3x the 12th's loss. The slice apportions in-window losses to regiments proportional to these rows (R-iv-regiment-apportionment), with the 12th's documented lightness preserved.

claims.value: When charged, nearly all that were left unhurt in three regiments surrendered (white handkerchiefs from the lying line); the 308 missing is the capture mass; the 12th NC held intact under Lt. Col. Davis

references.excerpt: When I saw white handkerchiefs raised, and my line of battle still lying down in position
references.interpretation: The surrender-and-exoneration record carried whole (dossier discipline: neither pole adopted).

references.excerpt: one regiment threw down their arms and surrendered
references.interpretation: The same capture mass read from the Union left (the flank volley).

references.excerpt: The Twelfth Massachusetts had a galling fire on the flank of this brigade at this time, which I think had a great influence upon its surrender
references.interpretation: The receiving echelon's mechanism for the surrender.
308 missing pooled at brigade line (ED-49 pattern).

claims.note: SURRENDER IS NOT REPRESENTABLE with the current action/clip vocabulary — carried as the slice's named T5 vocabulary gap #2. The slice window therefore ENDS at t=7050, before the capture event; the proposed film window extension to ~7500 (the charge, the handkerchiefs, Halsey's rally) is gated on that follow-up.

claims.value: Moved forward to the crest of the hill — the drawn 'BAXTER'S BRIGADE' bar at the Mummasburg-road wall; drawn=report agreement

references.excerpt: moved forward to the crest of the hill
references.interpretation: The report's crest line.
The drawn blue bar at (4007, 8424) — the wall trace EDI-1 passes through this point.

claims.value: The brigade opened on the advancing foes a most deadly fire, soon causing them to recoil and give way; repeated lines repulsed; engaged over two hours to ammunition exhaustion

references.excerpt: the brigade opened on the advancing foes a most deadly fire, soon causing them to recoil and give way
references.interpretation: The massed volley from the concealed wall, then sustained fire — the slice's dominant audio-visual event.
Relieved after 3 p.m. with ammunition exhausted — bounds the fire's duration.

claims.note: The pre-volley concealment (Iverson's 'concealed stone wall') has no current action/clip representation (no kneel-concealed hold) — named T5 vocabulary gap #3; the slice authors hold -> fire_by_rank.

claims.value: No brigade primary; B&M-repro 1,452 (hop-flagged); division primary 'less than 2,500' for both brigades

references.excerpt: We went into action with less than 2,500 men
references.interpretation: Division frame; the 1,452 brigade figure rides the macro track (carried from the phase file's authored strength, itself the B&M hop — dossier EC2 conflict recorded, unresolved).

claims.value: Cutler's brigade came in on Iverson's flank and opened so hot a fire that one regiment threw down their arms and surrendered

references.excerpt: came in on their flank, and opened so hot a fire on them that one regiment threw down their arms and surrendered
references.interpretation: The Union-left leg of the destruction; macro keyframe t=6300 carries the changed-front position (3850, 8200).

claims.value: A concealed stone wall at the woods' edge — the enemy strongly posted behind it

references.excerpt: the enemy, strongly posted in woods and behind a concealed stone wall
references.interpretation: Existence and character; the trace geometry is editorial EDI-1 (through the drawn Baxter bar, bearing 70°).

references.excerpt: a body of woods, surrounded by a stone fence
references.interpretation: Independent corroboration of the wall-and-woods position.

claims.value: Woods on the Oak Ridge crest behind the wall; the Union line posted in them

references.interpretation: 'strongly posted in woods'; band extent editorial EDI-2.
'a body of woods, surrounded by a stone fence'.

claims.value: Open farm fields NW of the wall (John Forney's farm) — the brigade crossed and died in the open

references.interpretation: 'J. FORNEY' farm drawn at (3614, 8637); the sheet draws the ground between the ridge and the bar as open field.
The dead lying in a distinctly marked open line implies open ground; surface class (July grass/pasture) is editorial EDI-2.

claims.value: The J. Forney farm buildings drawn on the 2 P.M. sheet at (3614, 8637)

references.interpretation: Drawn farm position; building massing is editorial EDI-6 (Codori-analogue prop massing, same convention as ED-13).

claims.value: Capt. D. P. Halsey (AAG) rallied and led the remnant's charge; the 12th NC held intact under Lt. Col. Davis

references.interpretation: Iverson and Rodes both credit Halsey; the macro t=8400 keyframe carries the rally. OUTSIDE the slice window — proposed film-extension scope and a named-individual observer candidate assessed (and recommended against) in the design doc.
Corroborates Halsey's rally.
